summary: Iteration 16 ‚Äî backend-only verification of the new gopossible.pl integration + route approval + per-stop address correction. 20/20 pytest cases PASSED in 12.7s. Coverage: (1) POST /api/transfer/create ‚Äî missing X-Api-Key‚Üí401, wrong key‚Üí401, oversize-but-junk bytes‚Üí400, tiny payload‚Üí400, valid XLS + correct key‚Üí200 with {transfer_code (6 alnum), qr_payload 'gopossible:transfer:<code>', route_id, stops‚â•100, expires_at}. (2) GET /api/transfer/{code} ‚Äî unknown‚Üí404, valid‚Üí200 returning {route, transfer} with all 132 stops, claimed_at populated on first fetch, lower-case code accepted. (3) GET /api/transfer/{code}/status ‚Äî no auth required, unknown‚Üí404, valid‚Üí200 with route_id/claimed_at/expires_at. (4) POST /api/routes/{id}/approve ‚Äî unknown id‚Üí404, sets approved_at (verified via GET /api/routes/{id}), second call refreshes timestamp (idempotent). (5) POST /api/routes/{id}/unapprove ‚Äî clears approved_at to null, unknown‚Üí404. (6) POST /api/routes/{id}/stops/{sid}/address ‚Äî empty body‚Üí400, unknown stop_id‚Üí404, real Szczecin address ('Wojska Polskiego 64, Szczecin') ‚Üí 200 geocoded=true with lat/lng inside 35 km of (53.4285, 14.5528) and persisted to DB; gibberish address ('qzx zzz qqq xyzxyz 9999, Atlantydaaa') ‚Üí 200 geocoded=false with lat/lng=null. (7) Szczecin geocoding bias ‚Äî both reference routes verified directly: route 8c0a5cbe 162/167 geocoded with 0 outliers >35 km; route 1145d1f9 163/167 geocoded with 0 outliers >35 km. The city-prior + viewbox + 35 km accept-radius in geocode_one is functioning exactly as specified.
action_items: Nothing blocking. Optional: server.py is now ~1098 lines (well over the 700-line guideline flagged in iteration 15) ‚Äî strongly recommend splitting into modules (transfer.py, geocode.py, manifest_parser.py, models.py) before the next feature lands.
critical_code_review_comments: _require_api_key correctly returns 500 (not 401) when GOPOSSIBLE_API_KEY is missing from the env ‚Äî this fail-fast is good; both 'no header' and 'wrong header' map to 401, matching the spec.
transfer/{code} marks claimed_at only on first fetch (idempotent on re-scan) and still returns the route ‚Äî this is the right UX for couriers closing/reopening the app.
transfer/{code} expiry handling: parses ISO with naive‚ÜíUTC promotion and 410 on expiry ‚Äî clean. expires_at is computed via timedelta(hours=24) which matches TRANSFER_EXPIRY_HOURS.
approve/unapprove use update_one with id-only filter and check matched_count for 404 ‚Äî correct (no false 404 if approved_at already had the requested value).

update_stop_address re-geocodes synchronously and stores lat=None/lng=None on failure rather than leaving the OLD coords, which is the correct behaviour (stale pin on a new address would be worse than no pin).

geocode_one has two local `_accept` closures (lines 282 and 321) ‚Äî duplicate definition; the second overwrites the first. Harmless because they're identical, but a future edit could create a subtle bug. Suggest deduping when server.py is split.

Minor: transfer_create does NOT validate that the manifest actually parsed a non-empty stop list before generating the transfer code ‚Äî but parse_xls_manifest already raises 400 on empty results, so this is fine in practice.
seed_data_creation: One TEST_iter16_transfer route + transfer code is created during the module fixture and DELETE-cleaned in fixture teardown. No persistent test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new backend endpoints (transfer create/fetch/status, approve/unapprove, stop address re-geocode) are live and behaving exactly per the spec in /app/docs/gopossible-integration.md. Szczecin geocoding bias is verified on two real 167-stop routes ‚Äî 0 pins beyond 35 km of the city centre on either route. LocationIQ key is in env and being used (paid tier, viewbox priority works). The test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration16_transfer_approve_address.py is the regression suite for the next iteration ‚Äî runs in ~13s end-to-end including a full XLS upload via gopossible auth.
rca_of_issue: N/A ‚Äî feature verification only, no bugs found.
